description: Cut an n8n research automation's LLM cost ~99.98% (~$20/day to ~$0.004/day) by replacing OpenRouter per-query web search with self-managed Bright Data research piped into LangChain. Built multi-agent automation workflows with n8n, Firecrawl, and ScrapingBee, cutting a task from 10–12 minutes down to 15–17 seconds. Delivered end-to-end outreach automation with AI message drafting, reply classification, and HubSpot CRM integration, reducing manual work from ~18–20 minutes to ~1 minute per contact.
achievements: Reduced n8n research automation LLM cost ~99.98%
Built multi-agent workflows cutting task time ~97%
Outreach automation reduced manual work ~95%
Integrated HubSpot CRM with lead/company creation via APIs
Self-hosted n8n locally with load-testing before production promotion

description: Engineered a single-shot Grok/SuperGrok prompt that outputs a complete article plus Yoast SEO metadata in one pass, powering 772 published brand articles with 293 rejected in QA. Built SGP-Clipper, a Chrome extension that pulls business-registry data into production prompts, cutting per-company processing from ~15 to ~7–8 minutes. Cut end-to-end generation time ~30% via pre-research prompt setup and multi-agent refinement.
achievements: Single-shot prompt powering 772 published articles with Yoast SEO metadata
Built SGP-Clipper Chrome extension adopted across intern team
Cut end-to-end generation time ~30%
293 articles rejected in QA for content/compliance reasons
Scraped and consolidated contact info for ~15,500 regional companies

description: Developed a YOLOv11 stacking ensemble for MRI injury detection achieving 75.17% mAP, 88% FROC, and 73.50% F1 score. Engineered an end-to-end AI system with a Next.js web application, secure API middleware, Appwrite database and storage, and a containerized inference server running in Linux, analyzing MRI scans in ~3 seconds. Deployed to Azure Kubernetes Service with logging, monitoring, and alerting. Collaborated with three university medical faculty members and co-authored a paper selected as one of the Top 10 concept papers under the DOST-PCHRD-funded MAGNUS H-Hub, presented at IRCITE 2026.
achievements: YOLOv11 ensemble: 75.17% mAP, 88% FROC, 73.50% F1 score
End-to-end Next.js + Appwrite + Linux inference in ~3 seconds
Deployed to Azure Kubernetes Service
Top 10 concept paper, DOST-PCHRD MAGNUS H-Hub
Presented at IRCITE 2026 (Computer Science category)

description: Built a dual Random Forest regression ensemble predicting flood probability and depth over a ~1 km grid across Pampanga, trained on Google Earth Engine flood-frequency data with a 6-feature geospatial dataset. Deployed Edge AI to Flutter app by converting models via ONNX to TensorFlow Lite for fully offline inference. Built multi-source data pipeline and Google Gemini-powered advisory generating hyper-localized storm-preparation checklists.
achievements: Dual Random Forest ensemble for flood probability and depth prediction
ONNX to TensorFlow Lite for fully offline Flutter inference
Google Earth Engine geospatial pipeline with 6-feature dataset
Gemini-powered hyper-localized storm-preparation advisories
~1 km resolution grid across Pampanga
